content.full_text:
Assembly California Legislature

THOMAS McCLINTOCK
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

September 19, 1991

Fair Political Practices Committee
P.O. Box 944001
Sacramento, CA 94244-4001

To whom it may concern:

The McClintock for Assembly Committee would like a ruling on the following:
1. Whether it is legal or not to have two or more candidate controlled committees (i.e. McClintock for Assembly whose main purpose is to re-elect Tom McClintock to the Assembly and California Tax Revolt whose main purpose would be to contribute to anti-tax candidates and initiatives in California).

2. If it is not legal to have more than one candidate controlled committee in this situation, can McClintock for Assembly be changed to the California Tax Revolt Committee and serve the two above mentioned purposes: 1) re-electing Tom McClintock to the Assembly 2) raising and contributing an unlimited amount of money to candidates and initiative campaigns in California.

3. If the McClintock for Assembly Committee can be changed to the California Tax Revolt Committee, what steps need to be taken. Or if a candidate can control two or more committees what steps need to be taken.

Our current committee is McClintock for Assembly, there is no California Tax Revolt Committee as of today. Thank you for your time and effort in answering our questions. If you have any questions for clarity on any of these questions please contact John Weems at (805) 388-7810.

Sincerely,

John Weems
